docs.title: What is MQTT?
docs.text:
MQTT is a message-broker application that Meshtastic nodes can connect to. By connecting to an MQTT server, all LoRa traffic your node sees is sent over to our MQTT server where applications can pull and use.

We use MQTT for collecting data from nodes across the state. From this data, we are able to populate applications such as Malla to provide access to packets, metrics, maps and more.

How to help without connecting to MQTT

If you would like to still help with contributing your nodes data but do not want to connect to MQTT, please set OK to MQTT to true.

This allows any MQTT gateway (a node connected to MQTT) to submit your data to our MQTT server. For more information on enabling OK to MQTT, check out Meshtastic's website.

docs.title: MQTT Settings
docs.text:
To make it easier to connect to our MQTT server, you only need to change the Host in your MQTT module as we use the default username and password.

Key Value Documentation Host mqtt.tnmesh.org mqtt/#server-address Username meshdev mqtt/#username Password large4cats mqtt/#password Primary Channel Uplink true channels/#uplink-enabled Primary Channel Downlink false channels/#downlink-enabled Ok to MQTT true lora/#ok-to-mqtt

Reminder

We use MQTT strictly for collecting and displaying data from nodes across the state. To keep the system running smoothly for everyone, we kindly ask that you leave MQTT downlink turned off on all public channels. This helps reduce unnecessary traffic on the server.

docs.title: Selecting a Topic
docs.text:
When selecting a root topic, use the topic that matches closely to the region your radio is located in.

For example, a radio that is located in Nashville, TN may wish to use the topic msh/US/TN/Middle since Nashville is located in middle Tennessee.

Region Topic East Tennessee msh/US/TN/East Middle Tennessee msh/US/TN/Middle West Tennessee msh/US/TN/West

docs.title: Nashville Meetup at 4Wall Entertainment, January 7th at 7pm
docs.text:
Friends in the Middle Tennessee area are holding their monthly meeting over at 4Wall Entertainment in Nashville, TN.

This event is a BYOB event so please bring what you would like to drink. Feel free also to bring nodes/laptops/tools as this meeting will have a classroom/lab vibe to it as well!

Meet & Greet at 6pm

An earlier meet and greet will happen between 6 - 7pm in the same location

  • Date & Time: Wednesday, January 7th, 2026, 7:00 PM CST
  • Location: 4Wall Entertainment in Nashville, TN
  • Address: 111 Airpark Center E, Nashville, TN 37217
  • Parking: See the entrance map for more information on parking

docs.title: Nashville Meetup and MediumFast Discussion at Game Terminal, December 10th at 7pm
docs.text:
Note

This event has passed.

Friends in the Middle Tennessee area are holding their monthly meeting over at Game Terminal in Nashville, TN where they will be discussing the switch-over from #LongFast to #MediumFast and the pros and cons of it.

  • Date & Time: Wednesday, December 10th, 2025, 7:00 PM CST
  • Location: Game Terminal in Nashville, TN
  • Address: 201 Terminal Ct, Nashville, TN 37210
  • Cost: Game Terminal is open to the public, but anyone under 21 is $10 admission. See here for more information about Game Terminal.

To learn more about the meetup and #MediumFast discussion, check out the NashMesh discord!

docs.title: Nashville will be switching to Medium_Fast on January 3rd / Beginning stages for supporting MeshCore
docs.text:
The Nashville area has decided to conduct the switch-over from Long_Fast to Medium_Fast on Janurary 3rd, 2026.

Why the move to Medium_Fast?

Check out this Meshtastic article to learn more: Is LongFast Holding Your Mesh Back? Better LoRa Presets for Bigger Meshtastic Networks.

Things to note as the date approaches:

  • Messages will be sent over Long_Fast about the switch-over leading up to Janurary 3rd.
  • Smaller infrastructure nodes will be left as beacons to announce the switch-over.
  • Medium_Fast is the current future state of Nashville. There are no plans for switching back to Long_Fast.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Setting Description Documentation LoRa Preset Medium_Fast radio-settings/#presets Frequency Slot 45

For any questions, please head over to the NashMesh Discord server, channel #medium-fast-discussion. If you would like to connect to MQTT to help provide information for the network, check out the MQTT guide.

docs.title: MeshCore
docs.text:
There has been more discussion around setting up MeshCore in the Middle Tennessee area. To help move forward with supporting MeshCore, additional nodes will be deployed during the infrastructure upgrade to Medium_Fast.

Want to help with setting up MeshCore?

If you have spare nodes/node parts that you would like to donate to help with setting up MeshCore in the Middle Tennessee area, please head over to the NashMesh Discord server, channel #mesh-core.

For general MeshCore discussion in Tennessee, check out the TNMesh Discord server, channel #meshcore-discussion.
